name: asynq (Simple Reliable Task Queue for Go)

auth.notes: Redis connection string; no separate auth layer

performance.notes: Redis-backed; low-latency task enqueue; concurrent worker processing

agent_readiness.notes: Go background task queue backed by Redis. asynq.NewTask('email:send', payload). client.Enqueue(task). asynq.NewServeMux().HandleFunc('email:send', handler). Scheduled tasks, retry with backoff, task deduplication, priority queues, timeout/deadline. asynqmon web UI for monitoring. Concurrent workers with rate limiting. Similar to Sidekiq/Celery/BullMQ for Go. Used by Go microservices needing background job processing.

gotchas: No task result storage built in
best_when: Go services needing background job queues (email, notifications, data processing)
Distributed task processing across multiple Go workers
avoid_when: No Redis available (use in-process channels for local-only jobs)
Need complex DAG workflows (use Temporal or Hatchet)
Very high throughput (dedicated MQ like RabbitMQ/Kafka may be needed)
summary: asynq is a Go background task queue backed by Redis. Enqueue tasks, process with concurrent workers, schedule delayed/periodic jobs. Priority queues, retry with backoff, deduplication, timeout. asynqmon web UI for monitoring. The Sidekiq/BullMQ equivalent for Go. Redis required. Best for Go microservices needing reliable background job processing.
